100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 14, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva, discussing disarmament and the ongoing tensions in Europe. In the United States, a severe heatwave gripped the Midwest, causing widespread discomfort and health concerns. Additionally, the British Empire faced political turmoil as the government struggled with economic challenges and labor unrest. Meanwhile, in the world of entertainment, the popularity of jazz music continued to rise, captivating audiences across the nation.
